\documentclass[10pt,conference]{IEEEtran}
\begin{document}

\begin{abstract}
We present a novel approach to automated code review using large language models.
Our method achieves 42\% reduction in review latency with no loss in defect detection.
\end{abstract}

\section{Introduction}
Code review is a critical quality gate in modern software development~\cite{rigby2013,bacchelli2013}.
Despite its importance, it remains time-consuming and inconsistent~\cite{sadowski2018}.

\section{Related Work}
Prior work on automated code review includes~\cite{smith2022,jones2021}.
Rigby and Bird~\cite{rigby2013} studied code review practices at large scale.

\section{Methodology}
We fine-tune a pre-trained transformer model on a corpus of 50,000 reviewed pull requests.
Each sample consists of a diff and the associated review comments.

\section{Evaluation}
We evaluate on a held-out test set of 5,000 pull requests from 12 open-source projects.
Results are shown in Table~1. Our approach achieves 42\% latency reduction ($p < 0.001$).

\section{Discussion}
\subsection{Threats to Validity}
Internal validity: the model was trained and tested on public GitHub repositories only.

\section{Conclusion}
We presented an LLM-based approach to automated code review.
Future work will explore multi-modal inputs including CI run artifacts.

\bibliography{refs}
